mission.title: grpc: gRPC Python logging "All log messages before absl::InitializeLog() is called are written to STDERR"
mission.description: gRPC Python logging "All log messages before absl::InitializeLog() is called are written to STDERR". This issue affects 17+ users.

steps.title: Identify grpc troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your grpc setup:
gRPC library — check your language-specific package (e.g., pip show grpcio, npm ls @grpc/grpc-js)
Check your build output and test logs for errors related to this issue.

steps.title: Review grpc configuration
steps.description:
Review the relevant grpc configuration:
gRPC Core changed its logging system to use absl logging as per https://github.com/grpc/proposal/blob/master/L117-core-replace-gpr-logging-with-abseil-logging.md.

steps.title: Apply the fix for gRPC Python logging "All log messages before…
steps.description:
Current approach to solving this:
1. Add `abseil-cpp/absl/log/initialize.cc` as a dependency
2. Call `absl::InitializeLog()` automatically to solve this problem for 99% of users
3. For 1% that may be calling `absl::InitializeLog()` themselves, provide an easy way to opt-out of our automatic absl
```yaml
@sreenithi proposed an environment variable, a viable option too.

For posterity, here's how the absl double log init error looks like:
```

steps.title: Confirm gRPC Python logging "All log messages before… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test grpc to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
Current approach to solving this:
1. Add `abseil-cpp/absl/log/initialize.cc` as a dependency
2. Call `absl::InitializeLog()` automatically to solve this problem for 99% of users
3. For 1% that may be calling `absl::InitializeLog()` themselves, provide an easy way to opt-out of our automatic absl log initialization. 

Some ideas on absl log init opt-out for the 1%.
codeSnippets:
@sreenithi proposed an environment variable, a viable option too.

For posterity, here's how the absl double log init error looks like:

Linking https://github.com/abseil/abseil-cpp/issues/1656
How can we comply mute `grpc`/`abseil`?
It spams until the Kernel crash.
